title: 3 Amsterdam - 3 Bruges - 2 Brussels - 1 Maastricht - 1 Amsterdam

hotels.hotel_3star: Holiday Inn Express Amsterdam - Sloterdijk Station

services.description: Fly in, pick up car at Schiphol

day_descriptions.Day 1: Fly into Schiphol, collect your rental car and park it — Amsterdam is best explored on foot. The Jordaan neighbourhood, the canals and Vondelpark reward an unhurried first evening.
day_descriptions.Day 2: A full day in Amsterdam. Anne Frank House, Van Gogh Museum, canal cruise or just wandering the nine streets. All optional.
day_descriptions.Day 3: Zaanse Schans windmills, Volendam harbour and a cheese farm visit. Back to Amsterdam for a final evening.
day_descriptions.Day 4: Drive south through Delft and The Hague before arriving in Bruges by evening. The medieval belfry and canal reflections are best seen at dusk.
day_descriptions.Day 5: A full day in Bruges on foot — the Belfry tower, the St John medieval hospital, the Begijnhof. One of the best-preserved medieval cities in Europe.
day_descriptions.Day 6: Day trip to Ypres. In Flanders Fields Museum, the WW1 trenches at Hill 62, and the Menin Gate Last Post ceremony at 8pm.
day_descriptions.Day 7: Drive via Ghent (half day — the Gravensteen castle, Graslei quay) to Brussels for the evening.
day_descriptions.Day 8: A full day in Brussels. The Grand Place, Atomium, Manneken Pis and the best moules-frites in Belgium.
day_descriptions.Day 9: Brussels to Maastricht via Leuven. Maastricht is the Netherlands' most Burgundian city — limestone caves, Vrijthof square, exceptional food.
day_descriptions.Day 10: Maastricht to Amsterdam. Return car at Schiphol. Fly home.

optionals.description: Ypres - In the Flanders Fields Museum

description: Amsterdam's golden canals, Bruges' medieval belfry, Brussels' Art Nouveau grandeur and Maastricht's Burgundian charm on a self-drive through the Low Countries.
